# Evidence Graph Visualization

## What
Add `wikihistory visualize <page> --format mermaid|dot` that exports the evidence graph as a diagram showing claim lineage, revision timeline, and citation flow.

## Why
Varia's output is JSON arrays. A fandom researcher reading 500+ events across 20 revisions can't see the pattern. A Mermaid graph turns "claim_softened at rev 12, claim_removed at rev 14, claim_reintroduced at rev 17" into a visual timeline. The signal — gradual softening before removal — is visible in one glance.

## Context
Read first:
- `packages/evidence-graph/src/schemas/claim.ts` — ClaimLineage, ClaimVariant
- `packages/evidence-graph/src/schemas/source.ts` — SourceLineage
- `packages/evidence-graph/src/schemas/evidence.ts` — EvidenceEvent with claimId
- `packages/cli/src/index.ts` — CLI dispatch

## Implementation
1. Add `wikihistory visualize <page> --format mermaid|dot` command
2. Mermaid output format:
   - Timeline: revision nodes in sequence (rev 11 → rev 12 → rev 13)
   - Claim edges: colored by event type (green = added, red = removed, yellow = softened)
   - Citation edges: dashed lines from claim to source
3. DOT output for Graphviz-based tools
4. Pipe to file or stdout — user redirects to their diagram tool

## Invariants
- Deterministic — same page + same format = same diagram every time
- No rendering — outputs text format only (Mermaid/DOT)
- Claim lineage graph, not full event stream — filter to claim events by default, `--all` for everything

## Acceptance
- [ ] `wikihistory visualize Earth` produces valid Mermaid output
- [ ] `wikihistory visualize Earth --format dot` produces valid DOT output
- [ ] Output renders correctly in github.dev Mermaid viewer
- [ ] Gate: build, lint, typecheck, test
